description: We have been using sematext for more than 8 months and we are having a great experience. This tool has helped us to fully secure our online infrastructure with its various components that are deployed in almost every field.And more over we have seen a growth in our business and this tool is one of the reasons for the same, We look forward for our relationship with sematext in future too.
author: Aaryan A.
pros: Detailed Log MnanagementBusiness Heatth Monitoring by a full monitoring of cloud and online servers.An Exceptional Customer Support team willing to help 24/7Application Performance Monitoring which cheack health of our deployed applications and check the performance bugs igf any and troubleshoot the problem for a better solution.
cons: The deployment of the tool was bit hectic in the begning but customer support team helped us with a dedicated team for us to completely setup and deploy the tool. Pricing can be lower.

review_title: One stop solution for infrastructure insights!
description: I used this to manage my infra and this has really helped me a lot to understand the usage pattern of the app and also helps in safely predicting when the load will be high and when it will be low. Finding out memory leaks for JVM has also been very easy when I was trying to get insights about GC and JVM pool.
author: Debodirno C.
pros: - Very detailed reports - Installing an agent happens in a jiffy - Very intuitive UI - Good support from the Sematext team
cons: - Documentation missing for old agents - Ubuntu < 16 - Agent once crashed - cause is still unknown!
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Worth switching from other logging aggregation
description: Satisfied but need more time to evaluate
author: Chao Y.
pros: I just switch from ELK. Sematext is a surprise to me. It is so easy to integrate and so easy to use. The web UI is so intuitive and the query syntax is so easy to use even if you are not a technical guy. Compared to ELK, it is a real cloud service, you never need to worry about you lose the logs .
cons: I haven’t found so far. Only thing is the price is a little bit high for a standup.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

description: We have been monitoring a fleet of IoT devices with Sematext. Using it we can quickly send data from the devices and analyze it later, making feature development faster and debugging way easier.
author: Lucas P.
pros: Easy to use and extremely fast to filter log data. Ready-to-use Java and Android libraries made integration a very fast process. Main UI is very similar to Kibana, and it also provides the full experience if you need it. Fast and knowledgeable tech support.
cons: There are rare instances where the UI becomes unresponsive or slow.

description: Sematext is a proven and reliable offering, without a single outage I can recall in the past 2.5 years. The customer support is amazing and queries are answered without fail within a couple of hours. They are constantly working on the product and increasing their offerings. They offer customers the chance to trial new products and give feedback, which is fantastic. We use Sematext for: * Log Aggregation * Monitoring and Alerting * Incident Investigation * Log Retention * Debugging I love how we can easily set up alerts on logs and receive Slack or OpsGenie notifications when events occur. I would highly recommend Sematext as a vendor.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: * Super Reliable * Excellent Customer Support * Constantly Adding Features * Constantly Improving
cons: There are very few things not to like about this software and company. The way accounts are set up, where you have to log in then "become" the account owner to see your logs is strange, and needs to be explained to each new user.

review_title: Advance and special next generation monitoring tool
author: Lawne P.
pros: Sematext let easily develop interfaces to retrieve logs for test application.The pricing model - the cost per month are exactly predictable. Sematext give us beautiful insights of Infrastructure by fetching logs of remote end point.
cons: The pricing structure is a little bit costly in my opinion and Needs more integrations with security tools for better optimally use.

review_title: Way more than a log tool
description: Sematex has become increasingly essential in our tool set. It goes far beyond a simple log manager, delivering valuable information from this huge amount of data.
author: Guilherme G.
pros: Sematex has impressed us in recent months for its ability to deliver robust tools for analyzing and mining the information present in our logs. Through its customizable Dashboards, we can analyze very important statistics both to characterize the behavior of our users and to help us identify problems in our code.
cons: The experience of using the Sematex interface has improved a lot, but it is not always so intuitive to find the resources we want. Reading the logs is also not as clean as I would like to see.

review_title: A cool managed service coming from seemingly technically versed people
description: Easy setup, no-headache operations, flexible and instant sizing and fantastic technical support
author: Szabolcs S.
pros: Fantastic technical support, even feature requests (for sematext-agent) were sometimes implemented within hours. Sematext even setup its EU data center (partly) on our request. Thank you very much!
cons: Business arrangements like a custom contract or plan sizes are not so easy and speedy to "implement" - but that's only a non-technical issue in the case of bigger probably public corporations.

author: Patrick B.
pros: - Choice of Sematexts own simple UI, and Kibana - Easy to integrate, since the API supports a number of common standards (elasticsearch, syslogd)
cons: - User Management could be improved, members added to the team have to switch to the team sphere, even if they dont have and apps configured themselves - Missing feature: Filtering the context view (e.g. if there is an exception, displaying its context works, but I cannot limit that context)

author: Michal B.
pros: The UI is quite easy to use, intuitive, does not reinvent the wheel.
cons: Setting up logging from a serverless nodejs app was quite complicated. I could not get the logsene-sematext library provided by Sematext to work and I could not find a solid documentation for how to use the log API myself. Luckily, the library is open source so I could figure out how to post log messages directly from the source code.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

description: Overall great experience and we use it daily. Have had no real issues
author: Justin M.
pros: Logs are sent quickly and the search features work great.
cons: Have ran into issues running out of log storage for the day which can impact production logging

review_title: Great but with a small caveat for what is probably an unusual setup
author: Matthew P.
pros: Super easy way to see metrics and do log analysis with alerting! We have clusters running in six regions around the globe and use Sematext for all our log analysis and error alerting.
cons: If you have a lot of clusters that you would like to keep separate in Sematext for various reasons (for us we have the same setup running in different regions of the globe) but want the same Sematext configuration (filters, alerts, etc) then you have to manually keep things in sync. Currently, it is impossible to have the same Sematext configuration applied to multiple apps...

review_title: Sematext shows one unified view for all of our Docker log, events, and metrics!
author: Ben R.
pros: Docker Metrics and Docker Logs all in one place! Correlating between logs, metrics, and events is awesome! Hosted Kibana without the hassle of setting it up ourselves.
cons: Sometimes the UI can be a bit cramped, and slow. Features of the UI, like getting a list of events when correlating is not as intuitive as it could be (little spreadsheet icon). Logsene searching has a learning curve. Having logs, events, and metrics on one page can be cramped, and sometimes results in the page becoming unresponsive.

author: Steven I.
pros: We are using Sematext as a logging solution for early detection of unanticipated error conditions, both server-side and in our front-end JavaScript. We needed a system that would provide enough context so we could debug any issues detected. Overall, Sematext does the job. By logging what's going right as well as what's going wrong, It's pretty easy to understand the broader context.
cons: It is a bit pricey, but not out of line with other products in this space.

author: David C.
pros: Great for monitoring Solr, with great memory use graphs.
cons: We wished the retention period was a bit larger for the free tier.

author: Clayton S.
pros: Very easy to use Good support staff Efficient and easy billing
cons: User interface took me a second to get used to

author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Sematext is very easy to navigate by providing a nice interface environment without complicated clutter. The documentation is also amazing.
cons: It needs more integrations with security tools.

review_title: Best monitoring tool under the budget
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Easy to setup and configure addons. Log Monitoring & Infra monitoring is good.
cons: None, it suited within our budget. You can add option for real time monitoring. The current poll interval is 1 min I guess.
author_duration: Used the software for: I used a free trial

description: Sematext support was overall positive and they are super helpful when integrating with the tech stack.
author: Guru S.
pros: Sematext provided support for our GoSchedule engineering team to track server logs, exceptions, and other essential metrics in a single dashboard. This helped the whole engineering team to decide when to scale and add more machines for production load. This increased our engineering team productivity and sleep well during night.
cons: So far we didnt have any negative experience with the software.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

description: I have a feeling of an easy to understand product, and that motivates people to adopt new platforms and the paradigm it represents.
author: Carlos M.
pros: I am impressed with how easy it is to learn, start using the product, and the more I use it the more I discover features that I did not know I needed and makes my life easier.
cons: UI sometimes is in my opinion kind of slow
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

description: Very positive, love the UI and ease of use.
author: Claire R.
pros: I really enjoy the flexibility Sematext allows
cons: Integration was less straightforward than anticipated
author_duration: Used the software for: I used a free trial

review_title: Sematext is an excellent monitoring and logging platform for Docker and Kubernetes
description: Excellent platform for monitoring and logging for Kubernetes.
author: Deepak V.
pros: Easy to install. Collects relevant metrics.
cons: Does not integrate with AWS web services.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

description: I have moved the company into docker containers. Semester has been very helpful to show what is happening with our container infrastructure.
author: jason B.
pros: Ability to drill down to see exact metrics. Ease of use.
cons: None. I like it. Trying to get company to commit to larger engagement of SEMA text.

author: Joseph H.
pros: The software was quick and easy to get up and running in our test environment.
cons: There were some glitches with their management console, a little to much email notifications.
author_duration: Used the software for: I used a free trial

author: Verified Reviewer
pros: surely sends logs to me on my heroku app
cons: Not supported Japanese language, English only
